Topic 6: Part 3 (how October 7th started the Iran and Lebanese war)

[158:37]Let's explain the conflict.

[158:41]So, we have actually been talking about this war for two and a half years, going all the

[158:47]way back to October 7th.

[158:50]On October 7th, as you know, Hamas invaded Israel from Gaza in the south.

[158:59]And what psychological impact did this have on Israelis, on the people of Israel?

[159:09]The pre-war security status quo was no longer tolerable.

[159:15]In other words, Israel had on its northern border, Hezbollah, 50 to 100,000 fighters

[159:23]with 100,000 rockets.

[159:25]On their southern border, they had Hamas, 50,000 fighters with a small rocket arsenal

[159:31]of their own.

[159:32]And for a time, it was thought that this uneasy situation was safe for Israel.

[159:42]But when Hamas invaded, it made it clear that Israelis were no longer safe as long as these

[159:47]militant organizations existed on Israel's southern and northern border.

[159:56]Allegedly, they killed 1,200 people during this music festival and all of these peace-loving

[160:03]Israelis in their kibbutzes and on their farms and at their rave.

[160:08]And Israelis are terrified.

[160:09]They say, clearly, this is an intolerable situation.

[160:14]We thought we were safe, but clearly we aren't.

[194:47]And what I mean by that is,

[194:49]you've got three major components of the MOU,

[194:52]like we talked about.

[195:03]the 60-day negotiating period.

[195:25]because it seems almost inevitable
